text:
You are building an admin product catalog page for an e-commerce team.

The catalog is read-heavy and should feel fast for day-to-day browsing, so avoid re-querying product data on every request.

Admins can trigger a "Sync latest catalog" action from the page when upstream ERP/PIM data changes (pricing, inventory, availability). After submitting, they should be able to continue working immediately, even if the product list is briefly stale.

The expected behavior is that product data is refreshed in the background and becomes up to date shortly after, across any views that depend on the same catalog data.

Implement the sync trigger as a regular HTML form that uses an inline Server Action in the page.
Use the cache tag name "products" consistently for catalog caching and invalidation.

In practice, sync jobs can touch thousands of SKUs, so operators prioritize a responsive admin experience and eventual consistency across catalog views over forcing every request to block on freshly recomputed data.

I’m locating the catalog page and the existing caching/data-fetching path first, then I’ll wire the sync action so it invalidates the shared `products` cache tag without turning the page into a blocking refresh.

text: The workspace is small, so I’m reading the app entry points and config next to see whether this is a Next.js app router setup and where the catalog page should live.

I have the shape now: a single App Router page, a local product source, and no existing cache layer. I’m checking the current Next.js cache invalidation API so I can implement the sync action in the non-blocking way the page needs.

I’m editing the page now. The implementation will cache the catalog data under `products`, and the sync form will use an inline Server Action that invalidates that same tag with SWR semantics so the action returns immediately instead of forcing a blocking refresh.
The core change is in place. I’m running a build now to catch any App Router or server action issues, and I’ll tighten the cache behavior if the compiler surfaces something.

text: I hit a build-only issue from `styled-jsx` in a Server Component. I’m switching the page styles to a plain `<style>` block so the route stays server-rendered and the form action can remain inline.
Re-running the build to verify the page now compiles cleanly with the server action and cache tag wiring intact.

text:
Implemented the catalog page as a server-rendered admin view with cached product reads and a non-blocking sync trigger.

What changed:
- Added shared catalog caching in [`/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx:1`](file:///home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx#L1) using `unstable_cache` with the `products` tag.
- Added an inline Server Action form in [`/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx:13`](file:///home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx#L13) that calls `revalidateTag('products', 'max')`, so sync invalidation returns immediately and updated data can refresh in the background.
- Built the admin catalog UI in [`/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx:19`](file:///home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx#L19) with a responsive table and sync card.
- Expanded the mock catalog data in [`/home/user/workspace/lib/db.js:1`](file:///home/user/workspace/lib/db.js#L1) so the page shows SKU, inventory, and status fields.

Verification:
- `npm run build` passes.

Note:
- Next.js updated [`/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json`](file:///home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json) to include `.next/dev/types/**/*.ts` during the build. I left that in place.

If you want, I can also wire the same `products` cache tag into additional catalog views so they all refresh from the same invalidation path.
